message:
fix(e2e/bench): honest concurrent-writers comparison (BENCH-FAIRNESS #126)

Only one section of comprehensive-bench had a genuine bias: the
bench_concurrent_writers fsqlite arm ran sequentially on a single
:memory: connection while C SQLite side used file-backed WAL with N
OS threads. The docstring at line ~1816 literally admitted this. The
resulting 0.10x–0.14x "fsqlite wins" at 2/4/8 threads were misleading.

This commit only rewrites the fsqlite arm to mirror C's pattern:
file-backed tempfile, N OS threads spawned via spawn_bench_task, each
thread opens its own Connection, tries BEGIN CONCURRENT (with fallback
to plain BEGIN), uses whole-transaction retry on BusySnapshot (matching
mt_mvcc_bench's proven pattern — per-statement retry can't recover a
poisoned MVCC txn). The 117 other :memory:-on-both-sides scenarios
are untouched — same backend on both sides is already fair methodology.

Honest numbers at 2/4/8 writers (file-backed, both sides real MT):
  - 2 writers:  C=105.6ms F=63.7ms   ratio 0.60x  (fsqlite 1.66x faster)
  - 4 writers:  C=155.8ms F=81.5ms   ratio 0.52x  (fsqlite 1.91x faster)
  - 8 writers:  C=307.1ms F=120.5ms  ratio 0.39x  (fsqlite 2.55x faster)

Summary shift: 50/2/68 avg 2.74x (with 3 bogus concurrent wins at
0.10–0.14x dragging the mean down) -> 51/0/69 avg 2.85x (honest).
FrankenSQLite still wins at concurrent writes — just by ~2x, not 10x.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
